title: Tapping to Lock In Ease with Receiving and Spending
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the habit of bracing when money moves and landing in calm, equal ease with both directions of flow
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 79 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the old tension around money moving in both directions, and moves toward a calm, trusting relationship with both receiving and spending as parts of the same cycle.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 79 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when spending a meaningful amount of money produces a contraction, or when receiving money brings guilt rather than ease.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 79 of a 12-week money programme. Week 12 theme: live abundance as normal. Day 79 targets the old habit of guarding money at the outflow and bracing at the inflow, and works toward ease in both directions.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once felt tense when money moved, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I used to guard every outflow, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried about each decision, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I now feel calm as money comes in.
Side of eye: I feel calm as it goes out.
Under eye: I trust my steady inflow and wise choices.
Under nose: I see spending as part of the cycle.
Chin: I enjoy giving and receiving with ease.
Collarbone: I let money move without fear.
Under arm: I stay relaxed and confident.
Top of head: I lock in ease with every transaction.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I trust money to move in perfect balance.
Side of eye: I receive with joy and no guilt.
Under eye: I spend and invest with confidence.
Under nose: I know every outflow invites new inflow.
Chin: I feel calm with money in motion.
Collarbone: I live in a smooth, endless cycle of wealth.
Under arm: I relax as abundance keeps growing.
Top of head: I stay at ease with every pound that moves.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 79 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
